conjecture_title: Informal question: can the D5-freeness hypothesis in Theorem 1.6 be dropped?
paper_title: Pure pairs. X. Tournaments and the strong Erdos-Hajnal property
summary: Theorem 1.6 of arXiv:2202.13977 shows that if a tournament H admits a numbering whose backedge graph has at most three edges and H is D5-free, then H has the strong EH-property; the D5-freeness condition excludes exactly the case where the three backedges form the backedge graph of D5. The authors leave open whether this condition can be omitted. A web search covering literature through May 2026 found no follow-up paper resolving this question.
search_queries: tournament strong Erdos-Hajnal property D5-free backedge graph Chudnovsky Scott Seymour Spirkl 2202.13977
tournament strong EH-property D5-free pure pairs backedge graph three edges
arXiv 2202.13977 citing papers strong Erdos-Hajnal D5-free tournament 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up resolving the D5-freeness question was found in the indexed literature. The related paper arXiv:2402.10782 (NP-completeness of finding forest-orderings of tournaments) cites the broader conjecture about forest-ordered tournaments and the strong EH-property but does not address the D5-free condition specifically. The conjecture is recent (preprint 2022, published 2024) and the absence of a follow-up is consistent with it remaining open.
